title: Demaine et al. Open Question on Token Sliding for Interval and Chordal Graphs
statement_text: Can determining whether two independent sets are in the same connected component of $TS_k(G)$ be decided in polynomial time for interval graphs, and more generally for chordal graphs?
context_text: Demaine et al. [8] asked whether the Token Sliding reconfiguration problem is polynomial time solvable on interval graphs and more generally in chordal graphs. Yamada and Uehara [21] had previously shown that a polynomial time transformation can be found in proper interval graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that the connectivity of $TS_k(G)$ can be decided in polynomial time when $G$ is an interval graph (Theorems 2 and 3), fully answering the interval graph case; also shows the problem is co-NP-hard and co-W[2]-hard parameterized by the size of the independent set for split graphs, a subclass of chordal graphs (Theorem 1).

title: Question 1
statement_text: For any integers $k$, $D$, for any chordal graph $G$ of clique-tree degree at most $D$, can the connectivity of $TS_k(G)$ be decided in polynomial time?
context_text: Motivated by the contrast between the polynomial time result for interval graphs (clique-tree degree 1) and the co-NP-hardness for split graphs, the authors introduce the clique-tree degree of a chordal graph $G$ as the smallest maximum degree of a clique-tree of $G$, and ask whether bounding this parameter restores polynomial tractability.
